stories.headline: SpaceX Begins Trading on Nasdaq in the Largest IPO in History as AI Labs Watch the Tape
stories.key_takeaway: SpaceX's $75 billion Nasdaq debut at a $1.75 trillion valuation — the largest IPO in history — set a fixed-price playbook that Anthropic and OpenAI plan to replicate for their own 2026 listings.

pull_quote.text: There was an incredible focus on educating investors very early, given the breadth and complexity of the company. And it also seemed to be announcing major things on a weekly basis — like the xAI and Cursor acquisitions, or the Anthropic and Google compute deals — so the dialogue was constant.

stories.article_excerpt: SpaceX hit the Nasdaq floor on Friday at $150 a share — an 11% premium over its historic $135 IPO price — and never looked back.

stories.headline: The Magnificent Seven Just Shed $2 Trillion as the AI IPO Wave Spooks Big Tech Holders
stories.key_takeaway: The Magnificent Seven's $2 trillion June wipeout reflects institutional pre-positioning ahead of $3.5 trillion in AI mega-IPOs, with Goldman arguing sidelined cash makes the selloff overdone and Standard Chartered warning of a real liquidity drain.

pull_quote.text: Bringing this $3.5 trillion in combined equity to the public markets would almost certainly require significant institutional recalibration.
pull_quote.role: Global Chief Investment Officer, Standard Chartered Wealth Solutions

stories.article_excerpt: The seven stocks that defined the AI bull market are now its biggest liability as capital braces for a wave of mega-IPOs.

stories.headline: Neura Robotics Lands Up to $1.4 Billion From Amazon and Nvidia to Mass-Produce Humanoids
stories.key_takeaway: NEURA Robotics' $1.4 billion Series C — backed by Amazon, NVIDIA, Tether and the European Investment Bank — is among the largest ever for a full-stack robotics company and positions the German startup as Europe's leading humanoid contender.

pull_quote.text: The future of AI will not only live on screens. It will move, interact, learn and work beside us in the real world.

stories.article_excerpt: A German startup from a small town in Baden-Württemberg just secured what it calls the largest raise ever for a full-stack robotics firm.

stories.headline: Anthropic Taps Tata Consultancy Services to Push Claude Deeper Into the Enterprise
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic named TCS a Global Premier Partner to build a dedicated Claude business unit and deploy the model to 50,000 TCS employees and its global clients, accelerating Anthropic's integrator-channel strategy ahead of its October 2026 IPO.

pull_quote.text: Enterprise AI value comes from understanding business context, orchestrating complex systems, and applying deep AI engineering talent. By combining Claude with our industry expertise, engineering rigor, and large-scale transformation capabilities, we will help customers move faster to production.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has enlisted India's largest IT-services firm as a distribution weapon for Claude across the global enterprise.

stories.headline: Cadence and Nvidia Expand Their Alliance to Close Robotics' Stubborn "Sim-to-Real" Gap
stories.key_takeaway: Cadence and NVIDIA combined high-fidelity multiphysics simulation with the Isaac and Cosmos AI stacks to create an end-to-end pipeline that makes synthetic robot-training data physically accurate enough to transfer reliably to the real world.

pull_quote.text: The more accurate the generated training data is, the better the model will be.
pull_quote.role: President and CEO, Cadence Design Systems

stories.article_excerpt: Every robotics engineer knows the ritual: a robot that navigates flawlessly in simulation promptly stumbles in the real world.

stories.headline: Claude Code Gains Nested Sub-Agents, Turning a Single Assistant Into a Coordinated Team
stories.key_takeaway: Claude Code v2.1.172 introduces recursive nested sub-agents, letting one session orchestrate parallel specialist agents on complex engineering tasks — backed by the Fable 5 model's 80.3% SWE-Bench Pro score.

pull_quote.text: Claude Fable 5 is the state of the art model on CursorBench. It's opened up a class of long-horizon problems that were out of reach.

stories.article_excerpt: For years the ceiling on AI-assisted software engineering was context: one model, one thread. That just changed.

stories.headline: OpenAI Acquires Ona to Give Codex Days-Long Autonomy on Real Software Projects
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI is acquiring Ona (formerly Gitpod) to give Codex persistent, customer-controlled cloud environments that let AI agents continue complex software-engineering work over days without losing context.

pull_quote.text: Agents need more than intelligence; they need a trusted workspace. We built Ona to give agents cloud environments with the context, control and collaboration enterprises require.

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI announced it has agreed to acquire Ona — the cloud-infrastructure startup formerly known as Gitpod — to power Codex.

stories.headline: Visa Plugs Its Payment Network Into ChatGPT, Letting AI Agents Check Out Anywhere
stories.key_takeaway: Visa embedded its global network and tokenized-credential infrastructure into ChatGPT, letting AI agents complete purchases at any Visa-accepting merchant within user-defined controls — the opening salvo in a payments-rails land grab for agentic commerce.

pull_quote.text: AI will transform commerce more profoundly than the internet or mobile technology ever did. As AI agents become active participants in the economy, Visa's focus is to ensure transactions are trusted, secure and seamless.
pull_quote.role: Chief Product and Strategy Officer, Visa

stories.article_excerpt: For decades the final step of any online purchase was a human clicking "Buy Now." Visa and OpenAI want to make that optional.

stories.headline: Nvidia Tells Chinese Customers Its New Vera Chip Can Be Ordered as Soon as August
stories.key_takeaway: Nvidia is pitching its export-compliant Vera server CPU to Chinese cloud customers with an August delivery window, opening a legal revenue channel while its H200 GPU business stays frozen by US-China regulatory friction.

pull_quote.text: Conceding an entire market the size of China probably does not make a lot of strategic sense. I think that has already largely backfired.

stories.article_excerpt: With its flagship AI accelerators still locked out of the world's largest emerging market, Nvidia has found another path back in.

stories.headline: O-Voxel: A New 3D Representation From CVPR 2026 Aims to Make Generative 3D Actually Usable
stories.key_takeaway: O-Voxel, a field-free sparse-voxel representation that jointly encodes geometry and PBR materials, won a top CVPR 2026 honor by letting generative 3D models produce usable, topology-agnostic assets that prior mesh- and SDF-based approaches could not.

pull_quote.text: The geometry and material quality of our generated assets far exceed those of existing models. We believe our approach offers a significant advancement in 3D generative modeling.
pull_quote.role: Authors, Native and Compact Structured Latents for 3D Generation

stories.article_excerpt: Ask a state-of-the-art image model for a photoreal landscape and you get it in seconds. Generative 3D has lagged far behind — until now.

stories.headline: New Research Tackles AI's Memory Problem: Toward Agents That Actually Remember
stories.key_takeaway: Two early-2026 arXiv papers — one auto-discovered through an AI research pipeline, one neuro-symbolic — give agents persistent, structured memory across long-horizon tasks, reporting large gains on memory and deductive-reasoning benchmarks.

pull_quote.text: Most existing multimodal agent memories rely primarily on neural representations and vector-based retrieval, which are well-suited for inductive, intuitive reasoning but fundamentally limited in supporting the analytical, deductive reasoning critical for real-world decision making.

stories.article_excerpt: Ask most AI assistants about a conversation you had last week and you get a polite blank stare. New research wants to fix that.

stories.headline: New York Passes a Sweeping AI Package: Kids' Chatbot Safety, Training-Data Transparency and More
stories.key_takeaway: New York passed a coordinated set of AI bills in its 2026 session — covering kids' chatbot safety, training-data transparency, AI news disclosure, a data-center moratorium and a surveillance-pricing ban — now awaiting Governor Hochul's signature amid a federal preemption standoff.

pull_quote.text: New York has a responsibility to ensure that innovation does not come at the expense of children's safety. For too long, tech companies have been allowed to deploy increasingly powerful systems without meaningful protections.
pull_quote.role: State Senator, Chair, Senate Internet and Technology Committee

stories.article_excerpt: Albany wrapped its 2026 session by doing what most state capitals have struggled to do: pass a coordinated sweep of AI rules.

stories.headline: Trump Abruptly Pulls an AI Executive Order, Citing Fears It Could Hand China the Lead
stories.key_takeaway: After Trump canceled a planned AI executive-order signing over China-competition fears and industry pushback, a narrower voluntary-review version was quietly signed in early June, underscoring the fight over federal preemption of state AI law.

pull_quote.text: Right now, we're working in a collaborative way with the technology companies, and we're just trying to make sure that the American people are as safe as possible.
pull_quote.role: Vice President of the United States

stories.article_excerpt: Everything was set for a high-profile Oval Office moment. Then the landmark AI executive order abruptly vanished from the schedule.
